metadata.description: Comprehensive construction symbol reference library organized by CSI MasterFormat divisions. Each entry describes the visual appearance of standard construction drawing symbols to aid vision-model extraction and classification.
metadata.usage: Loaded by symbol-learner.ts as baseline vocabulary. Relevant subsets injected into discipline-specific vision prompts during two-pass extraction (Gap 1). Visual descriptions help bridge vision model output to construction meaning.
metadata.confidence_baseline: Symbols matched against this library receive a +25 confidence boost in symbol-learner.ts scoring

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Compass-style arrow or stylized pointer labeled 'N' or 'NORTH'. May include a circle or diamond shape with directional indicator.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Placed on site plans, floor plans, and roof plans to orient the viewer. Usually appears once per sheet.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Look for prominent arrow symbol with 'N' label, often in corner or margin area of drawing.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Circle (typically split) with a line or arrow through it indicating direction of view. Contains drawing number on top, sheet number on bottom, separated by horizontal line.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Used on floor plans, elevations, and details to reference section views on other sheets.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Circle with internal horizontal divider. Top half = detail/drawing number, bottom half = sheet number. Arrow or thick line shows cut direction.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Circle or oval with drawing number over sheet number, connected by a leader line to the area of detail. May have a dashed boundary indicating the area covered.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: References enlarged detail views. Found on floor plans, sections, and elevations.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Similar to section marker but typically smaller. Look for dashed rectangle or cloud shape around the referenced area.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Circle (often with pointed triangle at bottom) containing drawing number over sheet number. Arrow or triangle points toward the face being viewed.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Placed on floor plans to indicate exterior or interior elevation views.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Circle with triangular pointer. Multiple elevation markers often clustered around a building footprint pointing in different directions.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Long dashed or chain line extending across drawing with a circle at each end containing a letter (A, B, C...) or number (1, 2, 3...).
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Structural grid system used on all plan views to locate structural elements. Letters typically run one direction, numbers the other.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Network of perpendicular lines with circled identifiers at ends. Usually extends beyond building footprint.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Cloud-shaped or scalloped boundary drawn around an area that has been revised. Usually accompanied by a revision triangle with number.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Indicates changed areas on revised drawings. Paired with revision block in title block.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Irregular bumpy/scalloped closed shape. Look for adjacent triangle with revision number (△1, △2).

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Small triangle (△) containing a revision number, placed near revision clouds and in the title block revision schedule.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Identifies specific revision number. Cross-referenced with revision schedule in title block.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Small equilateral triangle with number inside. Often appears multiple times on revised sheets.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Heavy dashed or chain line across a drawing indicating where the plan continues on another sheet. Labeled with adjacent sheet number.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Used when a floor plan is too large for one sheet. Text like 'SEE SHEET A-102' appears on one side.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Bold dashed line spanning width of drawing. Text annotations on both sides referencing other sheet numbers.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Small simplified building footprint in corner of sheet with the current sheet's coverage area highlighted or hatched.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Shows which portion of the overall building is depicted on the current sheet. Common on large projects.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Small diagram in sheet corner or margin. One zone highlighted/shaded to indicate current sheet coverage.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Graduated bar showing measurement at the drawing's scale. May be graphical (alternating black/white segments) or textual (1/4" = 1'-0").
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Appears near title or in title block. Critical for dimensional verification.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Horizontal bar with alternating fills and dimension labels. Often accompanied by text scale notation.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: X or + symbol with an elevation value (e.g., +100'-0", EL. 100.00). May include an arrow pointing to the specific location.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Indicates finished floor elevation or grade elevation at a specific point.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Cross or X mark with adjacent elevation number. Common at floor transitions, landings, and site grade points.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Triangle or arrow pointing to a horizontal line with elevation value. In sections/elevations, shows vertical reference.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Establishes vertical datum points on sections and elevations. References survey benchmark.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Right-pointing triangle touching a horizontal dashed line with elevation text. Multiple levels stacked vertically in sections.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Number or alphanumeric code in a circle, hexagon, or diamond shape connected to an element by a leader line. References a keynote legend.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Links drawing elements to keynote schedule listing materials, products, or spec sections.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Small geometric shape (circle, diamond, hexagon) with number inside, connected by leader to building element.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Text notation showing drawing scale ratio, e.g., '1/4" = 1'-0"', '1:50', '3/8" = 1'-0"'. Often enclosed in a box or placed below drawing title.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Every plan, section, elevation, and detail includes its scale. Multiple scales possible on one sheet.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Text with fraction or ratio format near drawing title. Look for patterns like X" = Y'-Z" or 1:NN.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Jagged or zigzag line indicating that a portion of the drawing has been omitted or shortened for clarity.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Used to shorten long elements (walls, pipes, structural members) that would otherwise not fit at scale.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Zigzag or wavy interruption in an otherwise continuous element. Two parallel lines with zigzag between them.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Bordered area (usually right side or bottom of sheet) containing project name, sheet title, sheet number, date, revision history, firm information, stamps, and approvals.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Every drawing sheet has a title block. Primary source of sheet metadata.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Rectangular bordered area at sheet edge with multiple text fields, logos, and structured layout.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Text label below each plan, section, or detail view with the drawing title, number, and scale. Often underlined or in a styled block.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Identifies each view on a sheet. Format typically: DRAWING TITLE (number) SCALE: X" = Y'-0".
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Bold or underlined text below each drawing view. Contains drawing number and scale notation.

Division 01 - General Requirements.description: Letter or number label at drawing edges (A, B, C along top; 1, 2, 3 along side) creating a coordinate grid for locating elements.
Division 01 - General Requirements.typical_usage: Allows verbal reference to drawing locations (e.g., 'See Zone B-3'). Common on large format sheets.
Division 01 - General Requirements.vision_hints: Evenly spaced letters/numbers along sheet margins, sometimes in small boxes or circles.

Division 02 - Existing Conditions.description: Drawn with lighter or thinner lines than new work. May be shown as gray, screened, or dashed. Sometimes labeled 'E' or '(E)'.
Division 02 - Existing Conditions.typical_usage: Distinguishes existing elements that will not be demolished from new construction.
Division 02 - Existing Conditions.vision_hints: Lighter line weight or gray-toned elements compared to bold new construction lines.

Division 02 - Existing Conditions.description: Dashed lines or elements with X hatching through them. May use a specific demolition line type or be labeled 'DEMO', 'REMOVE', or struck through.
Division 02 - Existing Conditions.typical_usage: Indicates elements to be removed during renovation/remodel work.
Division 02 - Existing Conditions.vision_hints: Dashed outlines of walls/elements, often with X pattern through them. Look for 'DEMO' text labels.

Division 02 - Existing Conditions.description: Wall shown in lighter line weight or with hatching/fill pattern distinct from new walls. Labeled '(E)' or 'EXIST.'.
Division 02 - Existing Conditions.typical_usage: Renovation plans showing which walls are existing vs. new construction.
Division 02 - Existing Conditions.vision_hints: Wall outlines with '(E)' label, drawn lighter than new walls on same sheet.

Division 02 - Existing Conditions.description: Bold or heavy line weight distinguishing new work from existing. May be labeled '(N)' or 'NEW'. On demo plans, new work may be shown dashed.
Division 02 - Existing Conditions.typical_usage: Renovation drawings where new and existing elements coexist.
Division 02 - Existing Conditions.vision_hints: Heavier/bolder lines than surrounding existing elements. '(N)' or 'NEW' labels.

Division 02 - Existing Conditions.description: Element marked with 'SALVAGE', 'RELOCATE', or 'S/R'. May show current and future positions with leader lines.
Division 02 - Existing Conditions.typical_usage: Items to be carefully removed and reinstalled elsewhere.
Division 02 - Existing Conditions.vision_hints: Element with text label 'SALVAGE' or 'RELOCATE' and sometimes arrow showing new location.

Division 02 - Existing Conditions.description: Heavy dashed or colored line separating construction phases. Labeled 'PHASE 1', 'PHASE 2', etc.
Division 02 - Existing Conditions.typical_usage: Phased construction projects showing work boundaries for each phase.
Division 02 - Existing Conditions.vision_hints: Bold boundary line with phase labels. Different phases may use different line types or fills.

Division 03 - Concrete.description: Filled/hatched circle (round column) or square/rectangle (rectangular column) in plan view. Hatching uses concrete stipple pattern (dots).
Division 03 - Concrete.typical_usage: Foundation and framing plans showing column locations at grid intersections.
Division 03 - Concrete.vision_hints: Solid or stipple-filled geometric shape at grid line intersections. Sized typically 12"x12" to 36"x36".

Division 03 - Concrete.description: Concrete section fill shows random dot/stipple pattern (poche). Distinguished from masonry (diagonal lines) and earth (varied dots with triangles).
Division 03 - Concrete.typical_usage: Wall sections, foundation details, retaining wall sections.
Division 03 - Concrete.vision_hints: Random scattered dots pattern (stipple/poche) filling wall or slab cross-sections.

Division 03 - Concrete.description: Dashed rectangle (below grade) centered on column location. Dimensions shown. May include rebar callout (#4 @ 12" O.C. E.W.).
Division 03 - Concrete.typical_usage: Foundation plans showing individual column footings.
Division 03 - Concrete.vision_hints: Dashed rectangle around column grid point with dimension strings. Rebar notation nearby.

Division 03 - Concrete.description: Dashed rectangle running along wall lines (below grade). Width dimension shown perpendicular to wall.
Division 03 - Concrete.typical_usage: Foundation plans showing footings beneath bearing walls.
Division 03 - Concrete.vision_hints: Dashed parallel lines following wall path below grade. Width callout like '2'-0" WIDE'.

Division 03 - Concrete.description: In section: concrete slab with stipple fill sitting on gravel/earth. Thickness noted. May show vapor barrier (heavy line), wire mesh, or rebar.
Division 03 - Concrete.typical_usage: Foundation and slab details, floor plan notes for SOG thickness.
Division 03 - Concrete.vision_hints: Horizontal concrete section (stipple fill) with thickness dimension, sitting on triangular earth pattern.

Division 03 - Concrete.description: Text notation: '#' followed by bar size and spacing, e.g., '#5 @ 12" O.C. E.W.' (Each Way) or '#4 @ 18" O.C. T&B' (Top and Bottom).
Division 03 - Concrete.typical_usage: Foundation plans, slab details, wall sections, beam details.
Division 03 - Concrete.vision_hints: Text pattern: #N @ NN" O.C. with optional E.W., T&B, or CONT. suffixes.

Division 03 - Concrete.description: Dashed rectangle connecting footings below grade. Cross-section shows concrete with rebar (circles for longitudinal bars, dots for stirrups).
Division 03 - Concrete.vision_hints: Dashed outline connecting column footings. Section shows rectangular concrete with corner rebar dots.

Division 03 - Concrete.description: Circle (plan view) or vertical rectangle (section) representing deep foundation element. Labeled with diameter and depth.
Division 03 - Concrete.typical_usage: Deep foundation plans where soil conditions require piers.
Division 03 - Concrete.vision_hints: Large dashed circle on foundation plan, often 24" to 60" diameter, with depth notation.

Division 03 - Concrete.description: Thin dashed or chain line in slab plan with 'CJ' or 'SJ' label. Shows where joints are cut to control cracking.
Division 03 - Concrete.typical_usage: Slab plans showing joint layout pattern.
Division 03 - Concrete.vision_hints: Grid of dashed lines across slab area, spaced 12'-15' apart, labeled CJ or SJ.

Division 03 - Concrete.description: Double line with gap or zigzag fill showing full separation between concrete sections. Labeled 'EJ' or 'EXPANSION JOINT'.
Division 03 - Concrete.typical_usage: Slab and wall plans where thermal movement requires full separation.
Division 03 - Concrete.vision_hints: Double parallel lines with gap, zigzag, or diamond fill. 'EJ' label.

Division 03 - Concrete.description: Rectangle with X through it (plan view) embedded in concrete element. Labeled with size and anchor bolt pattern.
Division 03 - Concrete.typical_usage: Foundation plans and concrete details for steel-to-concrete connections.
Division 03 - Concrete.vision_hints: Small rectangle with diagonal X, usually at column locations or wall connections.

Division 03 - Concrete.description: Section fill pattern showing diagonal lines at 45° (differs from concrete stipple). Represents masonry/block construction.
Division 03 - Concrete.typical_usage: Wall sections, details where CMU block is used.
Division 03 - Concrete.vision_hints: Diagonal line hatching at 45° in wall cross-sections. Distinguished from concrete (dots) by line pattern.

Division 04 - Masonry.description: Double-line wall with diagonal hatch fill or cell pattern showing block coursing. Nominal width noted (8", 12").
Division 04 - Masonry.typical_usage: Floor plans showing masonry partition or exterior walls.
Division 04 - Masonry.vision_hints: Wall with diagonal line fill or visible cell pattern. Width label like '8" CMU' or '12" CMU'.

Division 04 - Masonry.description: Section fill showing diagonal crosshatch pattern (two sets of diagonal lines crossing). Distinguished from CMU (single diagonal) and concrete (stipple).
Division 04 - Masonry.typical_usage: Wall sections and exterior details showing brick cladding.
Division 04 - Masonry.vision_hints: Crosshatch (X-pattern) fill in thin section, usually 4" thick on exterior face of wall.

Division 04 - Masonry.description: Irregular shaped outlines with stipple fill between, or random rubble pattern. Coursed stone shows regular horizontal lines.
Division 04 - Masonry.typical_usage: Exterior wall details, landscape walls, fireplace details.
Division 04 - Masonry.vision_hints: Irregular polygon shapes with textured fill, representing natural or cut stone profiles.

Division 04 - Masonry.description: CMU cell shown solid or with concrete stipple fill, indicating cells filled with grout. Rebar dot may be centered.
Division 04 - Masonry.typical_usage: Structural CMU walls where specific cells are grouted and reinforced.
Division 04 - Masonry.vision_hints: Within CMU wall pattern, certain cells shown darker/filled versus hollow cells.

Division 04 - Masonry.description: Horizontal band within CMU wall shown with rebar and grout fill. Labeled 'BB' or 'BOND BEAM' with rebar callout.
Division 04 - Masonry.typical_usage: CMU wall elevations and sections at lintels, mid-height, and top of wall.
Division 04 - Masonry.vision_hints: Horizontal highlighted course in CMU wall with rebar notation, typically at 4' intervals.

Division 04 - Masonry.description: Steel angle or concrete beam shown above openings in masonry walls. Labeled with size (L4x3x1/4) or rebar schedule.
Division 04 - Masonry.typical_usage: Masonry wall details and schedules above door and window openings.
Division 04 - Masonry.vision_hints: Highlighted element spanning across top of opening in masonry wall. Angle or beam profile in section.

Division 05 - Metals.description: Wide-flange (I-shape or H-shape) profile shown in plan view at grid intersections. Labeled with designation (W12x26, W14x48).
Division 05 - Metals.typical_usage: Structural framing plans showing column locations and sizes.
Division 05 - Metals.vision_hints: I-shaped or H-shaped cross-section at grid intersections. Text label like 'W' followed by depth x weight.

Division 05 - Metals.description: Single or double line between columns labeled with wide-flange designation (W16x31, W21x44). In section, shows I-beam profile.
Division 05 - Metals.typical_usage: Structural framing plans showing beam spans between columns or walls.
Division 05 - Metals.vision_hints: Line between grid points with W-shape designation label. Multiple beams shown as parallel lines.

Division 05 - Metals.description: Light line or dashed line between supports labeled with joist designation (16K3, 24K7, 28LH06). Shown as series of parallel elements.
Division 05 - Metals.typical_usage: Roof and floor framing plans using open-web steel joists.
Division 05 - Metals.vision_hints: Series of parallel lines at regular spacing (4'-6') with alphanumeric joist designation.

Division 05 - Metals.description: Wavy line or corrugated pattern in section view representing metal deck. Labeled with gauge and depth (20GA, 1.5" or 3" DECK).
Division 05 - Metals.typical_usage: Roof and floor sections showing metal deck on steel framing.
Division 05 - Metals.vision_hints: Corrugated or wavy thin line in section at top of steel framing. Gauge and depth notation.

Division 05 - Metals.description: Rectangle (plate) with circles at corners (anchor bolts) shown at column base in plan. Section shows plate thickness and bolt embedment.
Division 05 - Metals.vision_hints: Rectangle with 4 circles at corners surrounding column profile. Bolt diameter and plate size noted.

Division 05 - Metals.description: Solid black fill in cross-section views representing steel members. Distinguished from concrete (stipple) and wood (parallel lines).
Division 05 - Metals.typical_usage: Wall sections, connection details, any section cut through steel.
Division 05 - Metals.vision_hints: Solid black filled shapes — I-beams, angles, tubes, plates — in section views.

Division 05 - Metals.description: Hollow rectangular or round section. Labeled HSS (Hollow Structural Section) with dimensions (HSS6x6x1/4, HSS6.000x0.250).
Division 05 - Metals.vision_hints: Hollow rectangle or circle in section. Label starts with 'HSS' followed by dimensions.

Division 05 - Metals.description: L-shaped section in details. Labeled with leg sizes and thickness (L4x3x1/4, L3x3x3/8).
Division 05 - Metals.vision_hints: L-shaped solid fill in section. Label starts with 'L' followed by leg dimensions.

Division 05 - Metals.description: Arrow pointing to joint with horizontal reference line and weld type symbols (fillet triangle, groove V-shape, etc.). Size and length noted.
Division 05 - Metals.typical_usage: Steel connection details and structural details.
Division 05 - Metals.vision_hints: Arrow with tail connecting to reference line. Small geometric symbols (triangles, V-shapes) at reference line.

Division 05 - Metals.description: Beam-to-column connection shown with full-penetration welds and stiffener plates. Distinguished from shear connection (bolts/angles only).
Division 05 - Metals.typical_usage: Moment frame details on structural detail sheets.
Division 05 - Metals.vision_hints: Complex connection detail with thick weld symbols, stiffener plates between column flanges.

Division 05 - Metals.description: Diagonal line between column-beam intersections on framing plan. Labeled with member size. X-brace or chevron patterns.
Division 05 - Metals.typical_usage: Lateral bracing shown on framing plans. Critical for seismic/wind resistance.
Division 05 - Metals.vision_hints: Diagonal lines in structural bays forming X, V, or chevron patterns. Member size label.

Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.description: Parallel lines (end grain pattern) in section view representing dimensional lumber or timber. End grain may show growth rings.
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.typical_usage: Wall sections, framing details, any section cut through wood members.
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.vision_hints: Parallel curved lines in rectangular cross-section. Distinguished from concrete (dots) and steel (solid black).

Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.description: Double-line wall with parallel line fill or left open (no fill) for wood frame. Nominal width noted (2x4, 2x6).
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.typical_usage: Floor plans showing wood-framed partitions and exterior walls.
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.vision_hints: Double-line wall, typically thinner than masonry walls. Label like '2x4 STUD WALL' or '2x6 @ 16" O.C.'.

Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.description: Parallel lines representing floor or ceiling joists. Labeled with size and spacing (2x10 @ 16" O.C.). Arrow shows span direction.
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.vision_hints: Parallel lines at regular spacing with arrow showing span. Size and spacing notation.

Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.description: Beam labeled with engineered product designation: LVL (Laminated Veneer Lumber), PSL (Parallel Strand Lumber), GLB (Glulam Beam).
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.typical_usage: Framing plans for longer spans or higher loads than dimensional lumber.
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.vision_hints: Beam line between supports with label like '5-1/4" x 11-7/8" LVL' or '6-3/4" x 12" GLB'.

Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.description: Thin layer in section with face grain pattern (alternating parallel lines at different angles) or labeled 'PLY' with thickness.
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.typical_usage: Wall sections, roof details, floor assemblies showing sheathing layers.
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.vision_hints: Thin layer (1/2", 5/8", 3/4") in wall/floor/roof section with face grain pattern or 'PLY' label.

Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.description: Triangulated framework of members shown in elevation. Labeled with span, pitch, and bearing requirements.
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.typical_usage: Roof framing plans and truss elevations/schedules.
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.vision_hints: Triangular framework with diagonal web members. Span and slope noted. Often shown as single line in plan.

Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.description: Double or triple member shown above openings in framed walls. Labeled with size (2-2x10 HDR, 4x12 HDR).
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.typical_usage: Framing plans and wall sections above windows and doors.
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.vision_hints: Bold line or double-line above opening in framed wall. 'HDR' label with size.

Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.description: Short members shown between joists or studs, perpendicular to main framing. Labeled 'BLKG' or 'SOLID BLOCKING'.
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.typical_usage: Framing plans at bearing points, mid-span stiffeners, or backing for fixtures.
Division 06 - Wood, Plastics, and Compos.vision_hints: Short perpendicular lines between parallel framing members. 'BLKG' notation.

Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.description: Wavy or zigzag lines filling cavity between studs or layers in section view. Labeled with R-value and thickness.
Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.typical_usage: Wall sections, ceiling details, floor assemblies.
Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.vision_hints: Wavy/scalloped pattern filling wall cavity in section. R-value label like 'R-19 BATT INSUL.'.

Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.description: Stipple or foam pattern (small X's or dots) in a uniform thickness layer. Labeled with type and R-value (XPS, EPS, Polyiso).
Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.typical_usage: Exterior wall assemblies, roof assemblies, below-grade waterproofing details.
Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.vision_hints: Uniform-thickness layer with small X or dot pattern. R-value and thickness notation.

Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.description: Heavy single line or bold dashed line in section, typically on warm side of insulation. Labeled 'VB' or 'VAPOR BARRIER'.
Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.typical_usage: Wall and roof sections showing moisture control layers.
Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.vision_hints: Single bold line in wall/roof section adjacent to insulation layer. 'VB' or 'V.B.' label.

Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.description: Bold line (often with specific symbol like dots on one side) on exterior face of below-grade walls or under slabs. Labeled 'W.P.' or 'WATERPROOFING'.
Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.typical_usage: Foundation wall sections, slab details, plaza deck details.
Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.vision_hints: Bold or heavy line on exterior face of foundation wall. 'WP' or 'WATERPROOFING' label.

Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.description: Bold angled line or bent plate symbol at wall-to-roof, wall-to-window, or wall-to-foundation transitions. Labeled 'FL' or 'FLASH.'.
Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.typical_usage: Exterior envelope details at transitions and penetrations.
Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.vision_hints: Bent line (like a bent plate profile) at material transitions. 'FLASH' or 'FL' label.

Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.description: Bold line at top of roof assembly representing single-ply, built-up, or modified bitumen roofing. May show multiple plies.
Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.vision_hints: Top layer(s) of roof section. Label identifies system type (TPO, EPDM, BUR).

Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.description: Small filled circle or hourglass shape at joints between materials. Labeled 'SEALANT' or 'CAULK' with specification reference.
Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.typical_usage: Exterior envelope details, expansion joints, window/door perimeters.
Division 07 - Thermal and Moisture Prote.vision_hints: Small dot or hourglass at material joints in detail views. 'SEALANT' leader notation.

Division 08 - Openings.description: Arc showing door swing with straight line representing door leaf. Single line from hinge to strike side. Gap in wall at door opening.
Division 08 - Openings.typical_usage: Floor plans showing all door locations and swing directions.
Division 08 - Openings.vision_hints: Quarter-circle arc with straight line from wall. Gap in wall lines. Door number tag nearby.

Division 08 - Openings.description: Number in a circle, hexagon, diamond, or rectangle positioned near door. References door schedule for size, type, hardware, and fire rating.
Division 08 - Openings.typical_usage: Every door on floor plans has a unique identifier referencing the door schedule.
Division 08 - Openings.vision_hints: Geometric shape with number inside, positioned adjacent to door symbol. Sequential numbering (101, 102...).

Division 08 - Openings.description: Two door swings (arcs) meeting at center of opening. Both leaves shown. May be equal or unequal pairs.
Division 08 - Openings.typical_usage: Floor plans at main entries, conference rooms, loading areas.
Division 08 - Openings.vision_hints: Two adjacent quarter-circle arcs mirrored at center of opening.

Division 08 - Openings.description: Dashed line showing door panel sliding behind wall or parallel panel. No swing arc. Arrow may show slide direction.
Division 08 - Openings.typical_usage: Floor plans for pocket doors, barn doors, sliding glass doors.
Division 08 - Openings.vision_hints: Dashed line parallel to wall indicating recessed pocket. No arc. Arrow showing direction.

Division 08 - Openings.description: Dashed line across opening with 'OHD' label or specific symbol showing coil above. No swing arc shown.
Division 08 - Openings.typical_usage: Loading docks, warehouse entries, service areas.
Division 08 - Openings.vision_hints: Dashed line across wide opening. 'OHD' or 'ROLLING DOOR' label. Typically 8'-16' wide.

Division 08 - Openings.description: Three or four parallel lines in wall representing glass and frame. May show operable sash with arrow or special symbol for window type.
Division 08 - Openings.typical_usage: Floor plans showing window locations in exterior walls.
Division 08 - Openings.vision_hints: Multiple thin parallel lines within exterior wall thickness. Window tag number nearby.

Division 08 - Openings.description: Number or alphanumeric code in geometric shape near window. References window schedule for size, type, glazing, and U-value.
Division 08 - Openings.typical_usage: Every window on floor plans references the window schedule.
Division 08 - Openings.vision_hints: Tag shape with identifier near window symbol. May use letter-number format (W-1, W-2).

Division 08 - Openings.description: Series of thin lines representing mullion grid in exterior wall. Individual panels may be labeled with type codes.
Division 08 - Openings.typical_usage: Floor plans at glass curtain wall systems, storefronts.
Division 08 - Openings.vision_hints: Thin regularly-spaced lines in exterior wall, much lighter than solid walls. Mullion spacing noted.

Division 08 - Openings.description: Combination of curtain wall framing with door leaf(s). Shows frame profile and door swing within glazed system.
Division 08 - Openings.typical_usage: Floor plans at retail entries, lobby entrances.
Division 08 - Openings.vision_hints: Door swing within thin-line glazing system. Frame profiles thicker at door locations.

Division 08 - Openings.description: Alphanumeric code near door referencing hardware group/set. E.g., 'HW-1', 'HS-3'.
Division 08 - Openings.typical_usage: Door plans/schedules linking to hardware specification.
Division 08 - Openings.vision_hints: Small text tag near door, typically 'HW-' or 'HS-' prefix followed by number.

Division 09 - Finishes.description: Text block within a room showing room name and number. May be in a box, circle, or stylized format. Room number often references finish schedule.
Division 09 - Finishes.typical_usage: Every room on floor plans has a name and number identifier.
Division 09 - Finishes.vision_hints: Centered text in rooms: name on one line, number below. May be in bordered box. E.g., 'OFFICE\n101'.

Division 09 - Finishes.description: Code or number in a geometric shape (often diamond or triangle) within a room. References floor finish schedule (CARPET, VCT, CERAMIC, POLISHED CONC.).
Division 09 - Finishes.typical_usage: Floor plans indicating finish material for each room.
Division 09 - Finishes.vision_hints: Small diamond or triangle with code inside each room. Sometimes integrated with room tag.

Division 09 - Finishes.description: Code or symbol on wall surfaces indicating finish treatment (paint, tile, FRP, wood panel). May use arrow pointing to wall face.
Division 09 - Finishes.typical_usage: Interior elevations and finish plans showing wall treatments.
Division 09 - Finishes.vision_hints: Text or code with leader pointing to wall surface. Keywords: PAINT, TILE, FRP, PANEL.

Division 09 - Finishes.description: Height notation in rooms, often on reflected ceiling plans. Shows ceiling height above finished floor (e.g., '9'-0" CLG', 'CLG HT: 10'-0"').
Division 09 - Finishes.typical_usage: Reflected ceiling plans and room finish schedules.
Division 09 - Finishes.vision_hints: Height dimension with 'CLG' or 'AFF' (Above Finished Floor) suffix in room areas.

Division 09 - Finishes.description: Pattern or code indicating ceiling material: ACT (Acoustic Ceiling Tile) shown as grid, GWB (Gypsum) shown plain, exposed structure shown with framing.
Division 09 - Finishes.typical_usage: Reflected ceiling plans showing ceiling material types.
Division 09 - Finishes.vision_hints: Grid pattern (ACT) or plain area (GWB) on RCP. Type code label like 'ACT-1' or 'GWB-1'.

Division 09 - Finishes.description: Grid lines within floor or wall area showing tile layout, size, and pattern. May include grout joint lines.
Division 09 - Finishes.typical_usage: Enlarged floor plans and interior elevations at tile areas.
Division 09 - Finishes.vision_hints: Regular grid pattern within defined area. Tile size notation (12x12, 24x24, etc.).

Division 09 - Finishes.description: Alphanumeric code in circle or box on wall, referencing wall type schedule/legend. E.g., 'WT-1', 'A1', 'W3'. Shows assembly composition.
Division 09 - Finishes.typical_usage: Floor plans identifying wall construction type for each wall segment.
Division 09 - Finishes.vision_hints: Code in geometric shape on or near wall with leader. References wall type schedule.

Division 09 - Finishes.description: Symbol or notation at wall base indicating baseboard type. May reference finish schedule or detail.
Division 09 - Finishes.typical_usage: Floor plans and interior elevations showing base trim types.
Division 09 - Finishes.vision_hints: Small symbol or text at wall-floor intersection. 'BASE TYPE' or 'RB' (rubber base) notation.

Division 10 - Specialties.description: Small rectangle on wall with 'FEC' label or fire extinguisher icon. May show recessed or surface-mounted.
Division 10 - Specialties.typical_usage: Floor plans showing code-required fire extinguisher locations.
Division 10 - Specialties.vision_hints: Small rectangle on wall face with 'FEC' or fire extinguisher symbol.

Division 10 - Specialties.description: Thin lines showing toilet stall partitions with door swings. Pilaster and panel layout shown.
Division 10 - Specialties.vision_hints: Thin partition lines in restroom areas with small door swings. Typically 60" wide stalls.

Division 10 - Specialties.description: Small symbols on restroom walls: TP (toilet paper holder), SD (soap dispenser), PT (paper towel), GM (grab mirror). Referenced to accessory schedule.
Division 10 - Specialties.typical_usage: Enlarged restroom plans and interior elevations.
Division 10 - Specialties.vision_hints: Small labeled rectangles or symbols on restroom walls. Two-letter abbreviation codes.

Division 10 - Specialties.description: Diamond, triangle, or custom symbol with sign type code. References signage schedule for size, content, mounting.
Division 10 - Specialties.typical_usage: Floor plans and signage plans showing room signs, wayfinding, ADA signs.
Division 10 - Specialties.vision_hints: Small geometric symbol at door sides or corridors. 'SN' or sign type code.

Division 10 - Specialties.description: Small L-shaped symbol at wall corners indicating corner protection. Height and type noted.
Division 10 - Specialties.typical_usage: Floor plans in high-traffic areas, corridors, kitchens.
Division 10 - Specialties.vision_hints: Small mark at outside wall corners. 'CG' label or keynote reference.

Division 11 - Equipment.description: Specific outline shapes representing commercial kitchen equipment: ranges, ovens, sinks, prep tables, walk-ins. Each labeled with equipment number.
Division 11 - Equipment.typical_usage: Commercial kitchen floor plans and food service layouts.
Division 11 - Equipment.vision_hints: Detailed outlines of appliance shapes with 'EQ-' prefix numbers. Referenced to equipment schedule.

Division 11 - Equipment.description: Circle (front-load) or square (top-load) with 'W' and 'D' labels representing laundry equipment.
Division 11 - Equipment.typical_usage: Residential plans, hotel laundry rooms, multi-family layouts.
Division 11 - Equipment.vision_hints: Circle or square shapes labeled 'W' and 'D' in utility/laundry rooms.

Division 12 - Furnishings.description: Rectangular outlines along walls representing base cabinets (24" deep) and upper cabinets (12" deep, shown dashed above). Labeled 'BASE CAB' and 'UPPER CAB'.
Division 12 - Furnishings.typical_usage: Kitchen, break room, lab, and reception area floor plans.
Division 12 - Furnishings.vision_hints: Solid rectangular outline (base) with parallel dashed outline above (uppers) along walls.

Division 12 - Furnishings.description: Slightly wider line or outline extending beyond base cabinet depth. Shows counter overhang and material notation.
Division 12 - Furnishings.typical_usage: Kitchen and lab plans, reception desks.
Division 12 - Furnishings.vision_hints: Outline extending 1-2" beyond cabinet face. May show sink cutouts or equipment locations.

Division 14 - Conveying Equipment.description: Rectangle representing cab with diagonal X through it. Door opening shown on one or more sides. Labeled 'ELEV.' with capacity and stops.
Division 14 - Conveying Equipment.typical_usage: Floor plans showing elevator shaft and door locations.
Division 14 - Conveying Equipment.vision_hints: Rectangle with diagonal X in shaft area. Door opening at landing side. 'ELEV' or 'EL' label.

Division 14 - Conveying Equipment.description: Arrow within stairway indicating direction of travel: 'UP' with arrow pointing in ascending direction, 'DN' pointing descending.
Division 14 - Conveying Equipment.typical_usage: Every floor plan stair shows direction from that level.
Division 14 - Conveying Equipment.vision_hints: Arrow within stair outline with 'UP' or 'DN' text. Break line at mid-floor showing upper/lower runs.

Division 14 - Conveying Equipment.description: Parallel lines with steps pattern and directional arrow. Wider than stairs, with handrail outlines on both sides.
Division 14 - Conveying Equipment.typical_usage: Floor plans in retail, transit, and public buildings.
Division 14 - Conveying Equipment.vision_hints: Parallel lines with step pattern and direction arrow. Usually paired (up and down side by side).

Division 21 - Fire Suppression.description: Circle with dot in center, or circle with short stem. 'P' designation for pendant (hangs below ceiling). Coverage radius may be shown dashed.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.typical_usage: Fire protection plans and reflected ceiling plans showing sprinkler layout.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.vision_hints: Small circles at regular spacing on ceiling plans. 'P', 'U' (upright), or 'SW' (sidewall) designation.

Division 21 - Fire Suppression.description: Circle with upward-pointing triangle or stem. 'U' designation. Used in exposed/industrial ceilings.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.typical_usage: Fire protection plans in warehouses, mechanical rooms, parking garages.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.vision_hints: Circle with upward indicator. 'U' label. In open-ceiling areas.

Division 21 - Fire Suppression.description: Circle with special symbol indicating concealed cover plate. 'C' designation. Flush with finished ceiling.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.typical_usage: Fire protection plans in finished office/retail spaces.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.vision_hints: Circle with 'C' label. In finished ceiling areas where aesthetics matter.

Division 21 - Fire Suppression.description: Circle or rectangle with 'RISER' label showing where sprinkler supply enters the floor. Pipe size noted.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.typical_usage: Fire protection plans showing supply point for each floor/zone.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.vision_hints: Larger circle or rectangle labeled 'RISER' or 'SR', usually in mechanical/riser room.

Division 21 - Fire Suppression.description: Specific symbol (often Siamese connection icon) on exterior wall. Labeled 'FDC' with inlet size.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.typical_usage: Site plans and first-floor plans showing exterior FDC location.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.vision_hints: Symbol on building exterior labeled 'FDC' or 'FIRE DEPT. CONN.' with pipe size.

Division 21 - Fire Suppression.description: Vertical pipe symbol in stair towers labeled 'SP' or 'STANDPIPE'. Hose valve connection shown at each floor.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.typical_usage: Fire protection plans in stairwells showing standpipe locations.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.vision_hints: Pipe symbol in stair area with 'SP' label and hose valve symbol at landing.

Division 21 - Fire Suppression.description: Small valve symbol at end of most remote sprinkler branch. Labeled 'ITC' or 'INSPECTOR'S TEST'.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.typical_usage: Fire protection plans showing test connection at hydraulically remote point.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.vision_hints: Valve symbol at end of most distant sprinkler branch line. 'ITC' label.

Division 21 - Fire Suppression.description: Number on or adjacent to sprinkler pipe lines indicating pipe diameter. Main lines larger (4", 6"), branch lines smaller (1", 1-1/4").
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.typical_usage: Fire protection plans showing pipe routing and sizing.
Division 21 - Fire Suppression.vision_hints: Numbers along pipe lines. Larger numbers on mains, smaller on branches near heads.

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Oval or rounded rectangle representing basin, shown against wall or in countertop. Faucet symbol may be shown.
Division 22 - Plumbing.typical_usage: Floor plans in restrooms, kitchens, janitor closets.
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Oval or rounded shape at wall/counter with drain point. In restrooms and utility areas.

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Elongated oval (bowl) with rectangular tank against wall. Outline shows fixture footprint. May include centerline to wall dimension.
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Oval with rectangle behind it against wall. 15" from wall centerline typical.

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Semi-circular or D-shaped symbol against wall. Smaller footprint than water closet.
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Small D-shape or semi-circle against wall. Spaced typically 30" on center.

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Circle with X through it or circle with crosshairs. Labeled 'FD' with drain size. May show slope arrows pointing to drain.
Division 22 - Plumbing.typical_usage: Plumbing plans in restrooms, kitchens, mechanical rooms, laundry areas.
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Circle with X or crosshairs. 'FD' label. Slope arrows radiating outward from drain.

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Circle with 'CO' label or circle with arrow indicating cleanout access direction. Shows maintenance access point on drain/sewer line.
Division 22 - Plumbing.typical_usage: Plumbing plans along horizontal drain runs.
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Circle with 'CO' text near pipe junctions or direction changes.

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Triangle or arrow symbol on exterior wall indicating outdoor faucet/spigot. Labeled 'HB'.
Division 22 - Plumbing.typical_usage: Plumbing plans showing exterior hose connections.
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Small triangle on exterior wall. 'HB' label. Usually near landscaping or wash-down areas.

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Circle or rectangle labeled 'WH' with capacity (40 GAL, 80 GAL) and BTU rating. Gas or electric noted.
Division 22 - Plumbing.typical_usage: Plumbing plans in mechanical rooms, utility closets.
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Circle labeled 'WH' in mechanical/utility space. Capacity and fuel type noted.

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Lines with specific patterns: CW (Cold Water) = solid line, HW (Hot Water) = long dash, HWR (Hot Water Return) = short dash. Pipe size noted.
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Pipe lines with different dash patterns. 'CW', 'HW', 'HWR' labels. Size in inches.

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Line with 'S' markers at intervals or specific dash pattern. Size and slope noted (e.g., '4" SAN @ 1/8"/FT').
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Pipe line with 'S' markers or long-short-long dash. Slope notation. Flows to building drain.

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Line with 'V' markers at intervals. Connects to drain system and extends through roof. Size noted.
Division 22 - Plumbing.typical_usage: Plumbing plans and risers showing vent piping.
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Pipe line with 'V' markers. Typically smaller diameter (1.5"-3"). Runs vertically through building.

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Line with 'G' markers at intervals or dot-dash pattern. Size and pressure noted. Labeled 'GAS' or 'NAT. GAS'.
Division 22 - Plumbing.typical_usage: Plumbing/mechanical plans showing gas distribution to equipment.
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Pipe line with 'G' markers. Size and 'GAS' label. Runs to furnaces, water heaters, kitchen equipment.

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Various symbols: gate valve (circle with stem), ball valve (filled triangle), check valve (circle with arrow). Type and size labeled.
Division 22 - Plumbing.typical_usage: Plumbing plans at equipment connections, branches, and isolation points.
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Small geometric symbol on pipe line. Valve type abbreviation nearby (GV, BV, CV).

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Rectangle or special symbol in drain line from kitchen. Labeled 'GT' or 'GREASE INTERCEPTOR' with capacity.
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Rectangle in drain line between kitchen fixtures and building sewer. 'GT' label with GPM rating.

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Circle with X or target pattern on roof plan. Connected to storm drainage. 'RD' label with pipe size.
Division 22 - Plumbing.typical_usage: Plumbing and roof plans showing roof drainage points.
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Circle with crosshair on roof plan. 'RD' label. Storm pipe line connecting to storm system.

Division 22 - Plumbing.description: Small rectangle or specific icon against wall. Labeled 'DF' or 'WC' (water cooler). ADA unit may be noted separately.
Division 22 - Plumbing.typical_usage: Floor plans in corridors, break rooms, common areas.
Division 22 - Plumbing.vision_hints: Small rectangle at wall in corridor. 'DF' label. Often shown as hi-lo pair for ADA compliance.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Rectangular outline with single crosshatch or solid fill. Width dimension shown. Direction arrows indicate airflow.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans showing supply duct routing from AHU to diffusers.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Rectangular outline with airflow arrows. Dimension labels (e.g., '24x12'). Connected to supply diffusers.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Rectangular outline with dashed lines or different fill from supply duct. May use 'R' label.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans showing return duct routing back to AHU.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Dashed rectangular outline or different fill than supply. 'RA' or 'R' label. Connected to return grilles.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Rectangular outline with 'E' or 'EA' label. Different line type from supply and return. Routes to exterior or exhaust fan.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans showing exhaust from restrooms, kitchens, mechanical rooms.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Duct with 'EA' or 'E' label. Routes to exterior wall or roof. Found near restrooms and kitchens.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Single line with diameter dimension, or two parallel lines for larger sizes. Diameter noted (e.g., '10" Ø').
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans for branch ducts and flexible connections.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Single line with diameter dimension. 'Ø' symbol. Typically branches from rectangular main.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Wavy or zigzag line representing flex duct connection. Typically short runs from rigid duct to diffuser.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans showing final connections to diffusers/registers.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Wavy/zigzag line, typically 6'-8' max length. Connects rigid duct branch to ceiling diffuser.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Square with X through it (4-way diffuser), rectangle with lines (linear diffuser), or circle (round diffuser). CFM noted.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans and reflected ceiling plans showing supply outlets.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Square-X pattern, linear bars, or circle on ceiling plan. CFM value nearby (e.g., '200 CFM').

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Rectangle with parallel horizontal lines (louver pattern). Connected to return duct. Size and CFM noted.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans and RCPs showing return air pickup points.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Rectangle with horizontal lines inside. Connected to return duct. 'RA' label.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Circle with propeller/blade symbol inside or arrow showing discharge direction. Labeled with fan designation (EF-1, EF-2). CFM noted.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans and roof plans showing exhaust fan locations.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Circle with propeller symbol. 'EF-' prefix designation. CFM and static pressure noted.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Large rectangle with internal sections showing filter, coil, fan, and mixing box. Labeled 'AHU-1', 'AHU-2'. CFM capacity noted.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans in mechanical rooms showing primary air handling equipment.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Large rectangular equipment outline in mechanical room. 'AHU-' designation. Internal component sections.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Small rectangle or diamond above ceiling representing zone control box. Labeled 'VAV-' with zone number. CFM range noted.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans showing zone terminal units in ceiling space.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Small rectangle on duct line with 'VAV-' label. CFM min/max range. Controls symbol nearby.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Small circle or 'T' on wall, connected by control line to VAV box or equipment. Labeled 'T' or 'STAT'.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans showing temperature control locations.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Small circle or 'T' on wall, usually 48" AFF. Dashed control line to VAV or equipment.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Symbol across duct showing blade position. Fire damper (FD), smoke damper (SD), combination (FSD), volume damper (VD).
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans at fire-rated wall/floor penetrations and for balancing.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Symbol across duct at wall penetrations. 'FD', 'SD', 'FSD', or 'VD' label.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Rectangle with fan circle on roof or ground-mounted pad. Labeled 'CU-' or 'RTU-' (Rooftop Unit). Tonnage noted.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Roof plans and site plans showing outdoor HVAC equipment.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Rectangle with circle on roof or mechanical yard. 'CU-' or 'RTU-' designation. Tonnage/capacity noted.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Paired lines (liquid and suction) with 'RL' and 'RS' labels or specific line types. Size noted.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans connecting condensing units to air handlers or fan coil units.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Paired pipe lines with 'RL'/'RS' labels running between indoor and outdoor equipment.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Pipe lines with specific patterns: HWS (Hot Water Supply), HWR (Hot Water Return), CHWS (Chilled Water Supply), CHWR (Chilled Water Return).
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans showing piping between boilers, chillers, and terminal equipment.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Pipe lines with two/three letter abbreviation labels. Different dash patterns for supply vs. return.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Duct dimension annotation (WxH format: '24x12') with tapered lines showing size transition. Reducer shown as trapezoidal.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans wherever duct size changes.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Trapezoidal shape in duct run where width/height changes. Dimensions on both sides.

Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.description: Small curved lines inside duct elbows indicating airflow turning vanes for efficiency.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.typical_usage: Mechanical plans at duct turns, especially larger ducts.
Division 23 - Heating, Ventilating, and.vision_hints: Small parallel curved lines inside duct 90° elbows.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Circle with two short parallel lines extending from one side. Standard 120V convenience outlet. Height noted if not standard (18" AFF).
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical power plans throughout building.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Small circle with two short parallel lines. Most common electrical symbol on plans.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Duplex receptacle symbol with 'GFI' or 'GFCI' label. Required in wet locations per code.
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical plans in kitchens, restrooms, outdoor, garage, laundry areas.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Receptacle symbol with 'GFI' or 'GFCI' text nearby. In wet/damp locations.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Receptacle symbol with '220V' or '240V' label or additional line. Used for high-power equipment.
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical plans for ranges, dryers, welders, large equipment.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Receptacle symbol with voltage notation. Typically on dedicated circuit near specific equipment.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Receptacle symbol inside a square or with specific floor-mount indicator. 'FLR' label.
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical plans in open office areas, conference rooms where wall outlets aren't accessible.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Receptacle symbol with square around it or 'FLR' notation. Located away from walls.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Letter 'S' or 'S1' with a small slash mark. Connected to controlled fixture(s) by dashed curved line (switch leg).
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical lighting plans at room entries.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: 'S' with small mark near doors. Dashed arc line connecting to ceiling fixture(s).

Division 26 - Electrical.description: 'S3' symbol. Paired switches controlling same fixture(s) from two locations. Both connected by dashed lines to same fixture.
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical plans at hallways, staircases, rooms with multiple entrances.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: 'S3' label. Two switches connected by dashed lines to same fixture group.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: 'SD' or 'S' with dimmer notation. Controls light level of connected fixture(s).
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical plans in conference rooms, dining areas, lobbies.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: 'SD' label near door. Dashed line to dimmable fixture(s).

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Circle with solid fill or specific recessed symbol. Labeled with fixture type (A, B, C referencing lighting schedule).
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical lighting plans and reflected ceiling plans.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Filled or half-filled circle on ceiling plan. Fixture type letter nearby.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Rectangle (2'x4' or 2'x2') in ceiling grid. May show lamp configuration inside. Fixture type letter.
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Reflected ceiling plans in offices, classrooms, commercial spaces.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Rectangle fitting into ceiling grid (ACT) pattern. Fixture type designation letter.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Semi-circle or bracket symbol on wall line. Fixture type letter. Mounting height noted.
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical plans in corridors, restrooms, exterior walls.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Semi-circle or small symbol on wall line. Mounting height in notes.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Rectangle with 'EXIT' text or specific exit sign symbol. May include emergency lighting (battery backup). Shown with arrows for direction.
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical and life safety plans at exits, corridors, stairways.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Small rectangle with 'EXIT' label at doorways and corridor intersections.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Symbol with battery indicator or 'EM' label. Provides illumination during power failure. May be combined with exit sign.
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical and life safety plans per code-required egress lighting.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: 'EM' label or battery symbol. Located along egress paths per code spacing requirements.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Rectangle on wall with panel designation (LP-1, PP-A, MDP). Connected to branch circuits by home-run lines with slash marks (circuit numbers).
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical power plans in electrical rooms, mechanical rooms, utility areas.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Rectangle on wall with panel name. Lines radiating out with hash marks indicating circuit numbers.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Line from device(s) to panel with arrow and slash marks. Number of slashes = number of conductors. Circuit number noted at panel end.
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical plans showing circuit routing from devices to panels.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Lines with arrow toward panel. Slash marks across line (// = 2-wire, /// = 3-wire). Circuit # at arrow.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Two circles or coil symbols side by side. Labeled with KVA rating and voltage (45KVA 480-208/120V).
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical plans showing voltage step-down equipment.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Two adjacent circles or coil symbols. KVA rating and voltage transformation noted.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Circle with 'M' inside connected to equipment. HP rating and voltage noted. Disconnect switch shown nearby.
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical plans at HVAC equipment, pumps, elevators.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Circle with 'M' at mechanical equipment. 'HP' rating. Disconnect symbol (rectangle with handle) nearby.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Small square or octagon symbol where circuits junction. 'J' or 'JB' label.
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical plans where circuits change direction or split.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Small square at wire junction points. 'JB' label. Multiple circuit lines converging.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Line (solid or dashed based on concealment) showing raceway path. Size and type noted (3/4"C, 1"EMT, 2"RGS).
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical plans showing conduit routing, especially for larger feeders.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Line with conduit size and type notation. Solid = exposed/accessible, dashed = concealed.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Circle with fixture type letter on site plan, positioned on pole or building. Mounting height and photocell control noted.
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Site electrical plans showing parking and pathway lighting.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: Circles on site plan with pole locations. Fixture schedule reference. Photometric coverage shown.

Division 26 - Electrical.description: Circle with motion lines or 'OS' label on ceiling or wall. Coverage pattern may be shown dashed.
Division 26 - Electrical.typical_usage: Electrical and lighting control plans per energy code.
Division 26 - Electrical.vision_hints: 'OS' label on ceiling in rooms. Dashed circle showing coverage area. Connected to switch circuit.

Division 27 - Communications.description: Triangle or specific data symbol on wall. Labeled with category (CAT6, CAT6A) and jack count.
Division 27 - Communications.typical_usage: Telecommunications plans at workstations, offices, conference rooms.
Division 27 - Communications.vision_hints: Small triangle on wall. Data category label. Often paired with power receptacle.

Division 27 - Communications.description: Triangle with 'T' or telephone handset symbol. May be combined with data outlet as combo jack.
Division 27 - Communications.vision_hints: Triangle with 'T' on wall near desks. In modern plans, often combined with data outlet.

Division 27 - Communications.description: Triangle with 'TV' or coaxial cable symbol. Mounting height noted.
Division 27 - Communications.typical_usage: Telecommunications plans in conference rooms, waiting areas, break rooms.
Division 27 - Communications.vision_hints: Triangle with 'TV' label. Usually at 18" or 48" AFF depending on mount type.

Division 27 - Communications.description: Circle with antenna symbol or 'WAP' label on ceiling. Coverage radius may be shown.
Division 27 - Communications.vision_hints: 'WAP' on ceiling plan with dashed coverage circle. Spacing for full coverage overlap.

Division 27 - Communications.description: Room labeled 'TR', 'MDF' (Main Distribution Frame), or 'IDF' (Intermediate Distribution Frame). Shows rack locations and pathway entries.
Division 27 - Communications.vision_hints: Room labeled 'TELECOM', 'TR', 'MDF', or 'IDF'. Equipment rack outlines inside.

Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.description: Specific symbol (often a hexagon or special shape) at exits. Labeled 'PS' or 'PULL STATION'. Mounted 42"-48" AFF.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.typical_usage: Fire alarm plans at all exits per code.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.vision_hints: Symbol near exit doors at 42"-48" AFF. 'PS' label. Required within 5' of each exit.

Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.description: Circle with 'S' or 'SD' label, or specific smoke detector icon on ceiling. May show coverage radius.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.typical_usage: Fire alarm plans on ceilings per NFPA spacing requirements.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.vision_hints: Circle with 'SD' on ceiling. Spacing per NFPA 72 (typically 30' max between detectors).

Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.description: Circle with 'H' or 'HD' label. Used where smoke detectors would false alarm (kitchens, garages, mechanical rooms).
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.typical_usage: Fire alarm plans in areas unsuitable for smoke detection.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.vision_hints: Circle with 'HD' on ceiling. In kitchens, garages, loading docks, mechanical rooms.

Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.description: Symbol showing horn (audible) and strobe (visual) notification. Labeled 'H/S' or 'NAC'. Wall-mounted in corridors.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.typical_usage: Fire alarm plans in corridors, open areas, restrooms (strobe only) per ADA.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.vision_hints: 'H/S' or horn/strobe symbol on walls. ADA-required strobes in restrooms.

Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.description: Rectangle labeled 'FACP' showing main fire alarm panel location. Typically near main entrance for fire department access.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.typical_usage: Fire alarm plans showing panel location, usually in main electrical room or lobby.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.vision_hints: Rectangle with 'FACP' near building entry or electrical room. Central point for all fire alarm wiring.

Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.description: Smoke detector symbol associated with duct. Labeled 'DSD' or 'DUCT DET'. Shuts down AHU on detection.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.typical_usage: Fire alarm and mechanical plans at AHU supply and return ducts over 2000 CFM.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.vision_hints: Smoke detector symbol on or near large duct. 'DSD' label. Associated with AHU shutdown relay.

Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.description: Camera icon or triangle showing field of view. Labeled 'CAM' with designation number.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.typical_usage: Security plans at entries, parking, corridors, high-security areas.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.vision_hints: Camera icon or pie-shaped view cone. 'CAM-' designation. Coverage area may be shown shaded.

Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.description: Small rectangle on wall near doors labeled 'CR' or 'CARD READER'. Connected to security system.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.typical_usage: Security plans at controlled entry points.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.vision_hints: Small rectangle at door frame. 'CR' label. Typically 42"-48" AFF on lock side.

Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.description: Small symbol at door frame showing magnetic contact. Labeled 'DC' or 'CONTACT'. Monitors door open/closed status.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.typical_usage: Security plans at perimeter doors and secured openings.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.vision_hints: Small mark at door head or frame. 'DC' label. Wired to security panel.

Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.description: Circle with coverage cone or 'PIR' (Passive Infrared) label. Detects occupancy in secured areas.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.typical_usage: Security plans covering interior spaces during unoccupied hours.
Division 28 - Electronic Safety and Secu.vision_hints: 'PIR' or motion sensor icon. Coverage cone showing protected area.

Division 31 - Earthwork.description: Long-dash, short-dash line with 'EG' label showing existing ground surface elevation. Elevation values at key points.
Division 31 - Earthwork.typical_usage: Site plans, grading plans, and building sections showing existing terrain.
Division 31 - Earthwork.vision_hints: Dash-dot line labeled 'EG' or 'EXISTING GRADE'. Spot elevations noted along line.

Division 31 - Earthwork.description: Solid or specific line type with 'FG' label showing proposed final ground elevation after grading.
Division 31 - Earthwork.typical_usage: Grading plans and building sections showing proposed terrain.
Division 31 - Earthwork.vision_hints: Line labeled 'FG' or 'FINISH GRADE'. Compared against existing grade to determine cut/fill.

Division 31 - Earthwork.description: Curved lines connecting points of equal elevation. Existing contours = dashed, proposed contours = solid. Index contours (every 5th) are bolder with elevation label.
Division 31 - Earthwork.typical_usage: Grading plans showing existing and proposed topography.
Division 31 - Earthwork.vision_hints: Curved parallel lines across site with elevation numbers. Dashed = existing, solid = proposed.

Division 31 - Earthwork.description: X or + mark with elevation value to two decimal places (e.g., 100.50). Shows specific ground elevation at a point.
Division 31 - Earthwork.typical_usage: Grading plans at building corners, curbs, swales, and drainage points.
Division 31 - Earthwork.vision_hints: X or + with decimal elevation (XX.XX format). At key drainage and transition points.

Division 31 - Earthwork.description: Line where existing and proposed contours intersect (zero cut/fill line). Areas inside labeled 'CUT' or 'FILL' with depth.
Division 31 - Earthwork.vision_hints: Boundary line with 'CUT' and 'FILL' labels on opposite sides. Depth values noted.

Division 31 - Earthwork.description: Arrow pointing in direction of drainage (downhill) with slope ratio (e.g., 2% or 1:48). Shows surface drainage direction.
Division 31 - Earthwork.typical_usage: Grading plans, parking lots, sidewalks, building pads.
Division 31 - Earthwork.vision_hints: Arrow with percentage or ratio. Points downhill. Multiple arrows showing drainage pattern.

Division 31 - Earthwork.description: Circle with 'B-1', 'TP-1' designation showing where geotechnical investigation occurred. References geotech report.
Division 31 - Earthwork.typical_usage: Site plans showing geotechnical exploration locations.
Division 31 - Earthwork.vision_hints: Circle with 'B-' or 'TP-' designation. Cross-references geotechnical report boring logs.

Division 31 - Earthwork.description: Random dot pattern with small triangular shapes representing natural earth/soil in section views.
Division 31 - Earthwork.typical_usage: Foundation sections, site cross-sections showing ground material.
Division 31 - Earthwork.vision_hints: Random dots and small triangles pattern below grade line. Fills earth areas in section views.

Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.description: Long dash-dot-dot line showing legal property boundary. Bearing and distance labeled on each segment. May show iron pins at corners.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.typical_usage: Site plans and surveys defining legal property limits.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.vision_hints: Dash-dot-dot line around site perimeter. Bearing/distance labels (e.g., N45°30'E 150.00').

Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.description: Dashed line parallel to property line showing required building setback. Distance from property line noted.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.typical_usage: Site plans showing zoning setback requirements.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.vision_hints: Dashed line inside and parallel to property line. Distance dimension to property line.

Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.description: Parallel lines at road edge showing curb face and gutter flow line. Type noted (vertical, rolled, valley gutter).
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.typical_usage: Site plans and grading plans at roads and parking lots.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.vision_hints: Parallel lines at pavement edge. Spot elevations at intervals. Flow arrows in gutter.

Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.description: Rectangle with parallel lines showing individual parking space. Dimensions noted (9'x18' standard, wider for ADA).
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.typical_usage: Site plans showing parking layout. ADA spaces marked with wheelchair symbol.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.vision_hints: Rows of parallel-line rectangles. ADA symbol at accessible spaces. Count noted for code compliance.

Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.description: International wheelchair symbol in parking stall and on adjacent signage. Adjacent striped access aisle (5' or 8' wide).
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.typical_usage: Site plans at required accessible parking locations near building entries.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.vision_hints: Wheelchair symbol in parking stall. Striped access aisle adjacent. 'VAN ACCESSIBLE' at wider spaces.

Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.description: Parallel lines showing paved pedestrian path. Width noted (typically 5' min for ADA). Expansion joints shown as perpendicular lines.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.vision_hints: Parallel lines with perpendicular joint lines at regular intervals. 5'-6' wide typically.

Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.description: Circular organic shape for canopy (existing = solid, proposed = open circle). Caliper, species, and root ball size noted.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.typical_usage: Landscape plans and site plans showing vegetation.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.vision_hints: Circular organic shapes. Existing trees darker/solid, new trees open outlines. Species abbreviations.

Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.description: Line with X marks or specific fence symbol. Type labeled (CHAIN LINK, WOOD, WROUGHT IRON). Height noted.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.typical_usage: Site plans showing perimeter and security fencing.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.vision_hints: Line with X marks or plus signs at intervals. Fence type and height label.

Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.description: Heavy line with hatch marks on high side (earth retention). Height and type noted. Section detail referenced.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.typical_usage: Site plans and grading plans where grade change requires retention.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.vision_hints: Heavy line with short perpendicular marks on uphill side. Height annotation.

Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.description: Symbol or keynote on pavement areas referencing section detail showing pavement buildup (asphalt/concrete over base over subgrade).
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.typical_usage: Site plans identifying pavement types and referencing structural sections.
Division 32 - Exterior Improvements.vision_hints: Keynote or symbol on paved areas. References detail showing layer thicknesses.

Division 33 - Utilities.description: Dashed line with 'ST' or 'SD' markers. Size, material, and slope noted (e.g., '12" RCP @ 0.5%'). Flow direction arrow.
Division 33 - Utilities.typical_usage: Utility plans showing storm water collection and conveyance.
Division 33 - Utilities.vision_hints: Dashed line with 'ST' markers. Pipe size, slope, and invert elevations at structures.

Division 33 - Utilities.description: Dashed line with 'SS' markers. Size, material, slope, and invert elevations noted. Flow arrows show direction.
Division 33 - Utilities.typical_usage: Utility plans showing sanitary sewer collection to municipal system.
Division 33 - Utilities.vision_hints: Dashed line with 'SS' markers. Invert elevations at manholes. Flows downhill to main.

Division 33 - Utilities.description: Solid line with 'W' markers. Size noted (6", 8", 12"). Shows fire hydrant tees, valve locations, and service taps.
Division 33 - Utilities.typical_usage: Utility plans showing domestic and fire water supply.
Division 33 - Utilities.vision_hints: Solid line with 'W' markers. Size labels. Fire hydrant symbols along route.

Division 33 - Utilities.description: Circle with arms (stylized hydrant shape) connected to water main. Spacing per fire code (typically 300'-600' apart).
Division 33 - Utilities.typical_usage: Site and utility plans showing fire protection water supply points.
Division 33 - Utilities.vision_hints: Distinctive hydrant icon on or near water main. Gate valve symbol at tee connection.

Division 33 - Utilities.description: Circle with cross or MH designation. Rim and invert elevations noted. Pipe sizes entering and leaving shown.
Division 33 - Utilities.typical_usage: Utility plans at pipe junctions, direction changes, and access points.
Division 33 - Utilities.vision_hints: Circle with 'MH-' designation. Rim elevation on top, invert elevations for each pipe connection.

Division 33 - Utilities.description: Square with diagonal lines or specific inlet symbol. Type noted (curb inlet, area drain, trench drain). Rim and invert elevations.
Division 33 - Utilities.typical_usage: Utility and grading plans at low points for storm water collection.
Division 33 - Utilities.vision_hints: Square with diagonal lines at pavement low points. 'CB-' or 'INLET' designation. Elevations noted.

Division 33 - Utilities.description: Line with 'G' markers or specific gas line symbol. Size and pressure noted. Gas meter symbol at building entry.
Division 33 - Utilities.typical_usage: Utility plans showing gas service from main to building.
Division 33 - Utilities.vision_hints: Line with 'G' markers. Gas meter symbol (rectangle) at building exterior.

Division 33 - Utilities.description: Line with 'E' markers showing underground electric service. Transformer pad or pole shown. Conduit size noted.
Division 33 - Utilities.typical_usage: Site utility plans showing electric service routing.
Division 33 - Utilities.vision_hints: Line with 'E' markers. Transformer rectangle with 'XFMR' label. Conduit size annotation.

Division 33 - Utilities.description: Line with 'T' or 'FO' markers showing underground communication service. Handhole/pullbox symbols at intervals.
Division 33 - Utilities.typical_usage: Site utility plans showing telecommunications routing.
Division 33 - Utilities.vision_hints: Line with 'T' or 'FO' markers. Handhole rectangles at direction changes.
