Division 33 - Utilities.description: Irregular shape on site with contour lines showing pond grading. Inlet and outlet pipe connections. Volume calculations noted.
Division 33 - Utilities.typical_usage: Site and grading plans showing stormwater management facilities.
Division 33 - Utilities.vision_hints: Irregular enclosed shape with internal contours. Inlet/outlet pipes. Wet or dry pond notation.

Division 33 - Utilities.description: Dashed boundary line with 'U.E.' label and width noted (typically 10'-20' wide). Restricts building within easement.
Division 33 - Utilities.typical_usage: Site plans showing utility easement corridors.
Division 33 - Utilities.vision_hints: Dashed parallel lines with 'U.E.' or 'UTILITY EASEMENT' label and width dimension.

Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).description: Random scattered dots/stipple pattern filling concrete cross-sections.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).typical_usage: Any section cut through concrete elements (walls, slabs, columns, footings).
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).vision_hints: Random dots/specks, not organized in a pattern. Distinguished from earth (triangles+dots) and gravel (large dots).

Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).description: Diagonal lines at 45° for CMU. Crosshatch (two directions) for brick. Spacing and angle varies by convention.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).vision_hints: Regular diagonal lines (CMU) or cross-diagonal lines (brick) at consistent spacing.

Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).description: Solid black fill for structural steel sections.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).typical_usage: Section cuts through steel beams, columns, angles, plates.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).vision_hints: Completely solid black fill. Small cross-sections = angles/plates. I-shape = wide flange.

Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).description: Parallel curved lines representing wood grain pattern in cross-section.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).typical_usage: Section cuts through dimensional lumber, timber, engineered wood.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).vision_hints: Curved parallel lines within rectangular section. May show growth ring pattern.

Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).description: Random dots with small triangular shapes scattered throughout. Coarser pattern than concrete.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).typical_usage: Section views below grade showing undisturbed or fill earth.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).vision_hints: Dots with small triangles. Coarser and more varied than concrete stipple pattern.

Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).description: Large irregular circles or dots representing crushed stone or gravel base material.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).typical_usage: Section views showing gravel base under slabs, behind retaining walls.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).vision_hints: Large irregular dots/circles, much bigger than concrete stipple. Under slabs and behind walls.

Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).description: Fine dots in a uniform pattern, finer than concrete stipple.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).typical_usage: Section views showing sand bedding for pipes or under slabs.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).vision_hints: Very fine, uniform dot pattern. Typically thin layer (2"-4") under pipe or slab.

Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).typical_usage: Wall, ceiling, and floor sections showing thermal insulation.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).vision_hints: Repeating wave/scallop pattern in wall cavities. R-value label nearby.

Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).description: Small X's or stipple in uniform layer, typically 1"-4" thick.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).typical_usage: Exterior wall, roof, and foundation sections showing rigid board insulation.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).vision_hints: Uniform layer with small X pattern. Thinner than batt cavities. On exterior face.

Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).description: Thin line(s) representing glass pane in section. Single line for single glazing, two lines for insulated glazing.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).vision_hints: Very thin lines in window/door frame sections. Two parallel thin lines = insulated glass unit.

Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).description: Thin layer with fine stipple or left unfilled with thickness noted (1/2", 5/8"). Often shown as simple lines on wall faces.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).typical_usage: Wall and ceiling sections showing drywall finish layers.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).vision_hints: Thin layers (1/2" or 5/8") on stud faces. May be double-layer (fire-rated). Simple thin line representation.

Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).description: Small square grid pattern on surface or thin layer with tile pattern in section.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).typical_usage: Floor and wall details showing tile installation.
Common Hatch Patterns (Section Fills).vision_hints: Grid pattern on surface in elevation. Thin layer over substrate in section.

Common Line Types.description: Continuous solid line of medium weight showing visible edges of objects.
Common Line Types.typical_usage: All visible edges, walls, equipment outlines.
Common Line Types.vision_hints: Standard solid line. Most common line on any drawing.

Common Line Types.description: Short dashed line showing edges hidden behind other objects. Also used for elements below the cut plane (below-grade in plan).
Common Line Types.typical_usage: Items behind or below visible surfaces: foundations in plan, beams above in RCP.
Common Line Types.vision_hints: Even short dashes. Shows items you can't directly see from view angle.

Common Line Types.description: Long-short-long dash pattern showing centerline of symmetric elements. Labeled 'CL' or with center line symbol.
Common Line Types.typical_usage: Column grids, pipe centerlines, equipment centerlines, symmetry lines.
Common Line Types.vision_hints: Long-short-long dash pattern. 'CL' symbol. Through center of symmetric elements.

Common Line Types.description: Long-dash-dot-dot pattern specifically for legal property boundaries.
Common Line Types.vision_hints: Long dash followed by two short dashes/dots. Around site perimeter.

Common Line Types.description: Heavy long-dash line showing where a section cut occurs. Ends with section markers showing direction of view.
Common Line Types.typical_usage: Floor plans showing where section views are taken.
Common Line Types.vision_hints: Heavy dashed line across plan with section bubbles at ends. Arrows show viewing direction.

Common Line Types.description: Long chain (center line type) with circle bubbles at ends containing grid identifier (A, B, 1, 2...).
Common Line Types.typical_usage: All structural and architectural plans for element location reference.
Common Line Types.vision_hints: Perpendicular network of lines with circled identifiers at ends.

Accessibility (ADA) Symbols.description: Wheelchair icon (ISA) indicating accessible route, parking, restroom, or entrance.
Accessibility (ADA) Symbols.typical_usage: Floor plans, site plans at accessible features per ADA/ABA requirements.
Accessibility (ADA) Symbols.vision_hints: Stylized wheelchair figure. Found at accessible parking, restrooms, entries, routes.

Accessibility (ADA) Symbols.description: Path marked with wheelchair symbol and/or dashed boundary showing accessible travel route. Min 36" wide, max 2% cross-slope.
Accessibility (ADA) Symbols.typical_usage: Site plans and floor plans showing compliant pedestrian paths.
Accessibility (ADA) Symbols.vision_hints: Path with wheelchair symbols. Width dimensions. Slope percentages noted.

Accessibility (ADA) Symbols.description: Dot pattern area at curb ramps and platform edges showing truncated dome surface.
Accessibility (ADA) Symbols.typical_usage: Site plans at curb ramps, transit platforms, hazardous vehicular areas.
Accessibility (ADA) Symbols.vision_hints: Dot grid pattern at transitions between pedestrian and vehicular surfaces.

Accessibility (ADA) Symbols.description: Thick line on restroom walls showing grab bar locations. Length and height dimensions noted per ADA.
Accessibility (ADA) Symbols.typical_usage: Enlarged restroom plans and elevations at accessible toilet and shower stalls.
Accessibility (ADA) Symbols.vision_hints: Bold line segments on walls near toilet and shower. Length (42", 36") and height (33"-36") noted.

Life Safety / Egress Symbols.description: Door symbol with 'EXIT' label or arrow. Required hardware noted (panic bar, closer). Fire rating if applicable.
Life Safety / Egress Symbols.typical_usage: Life safety plans showing required egress doors.
Life Safety / Egress Symbols.vision_hints: Door with 'EXIT' text or exit sign symbol above. Panic hardware notation.

Life Safety / Egress Symbols.symbol_name: Area of Rescue Assistance / Area of Refuge
Life Safety / Egress Symbols.description: Wheelchair symbol with specific area boundary at stairs or elevators. Two-way communication device shown.
Life Safety / Egress Symbols.typical_usage: Life safety plans at stairway landings in multi-story buildings.
Life Safety / Egress Symbols.vision_hints: Wheelchair symbol near stairs with 'AREA OF RESCUE ASSISTANCE' label. Communication device symbol.

Life Safety / Egress Symbols.description: Wall shown with fire rating notation (1-HR, 2-HR). Special symbol or heavier line weight. 'F.R.' label.
Life Safety / Egress Symbols.typical_usage: Life safety and architectural plans showing fire-rated separations.
Life Safety / Egress Symbols.vision_hints: Wall with fire rating text (1-HR, 2-HR FIRE BARRIER). May use special fill or thicker lines.

Life Safety / Egress Symbols.description: Symbol or text on wall indicating fire-resistance rating. '1 HR', '2 HR', or fire symbol with rating.
Life Safety / Egress Symbols.typical_usage: Floor plans and life safety plans identifying fire-rated construction.
Life Safety / Egress Symbols.vision_hints: Small tag or text on walls showing hour rating. May be integrated with wall type tag.

Life Safety / Egress Symbols.description: Dimension line or dashed path showing distance from most remote point to nearest exit. Length noted per code maximums.
Life Safety / Egress Symbols.typical_usage: Life safety plans demonstrating code compliance for egress travel distance.
Life Safety / Egress Symbols.vision_hints: Dashed path line with dimension from remote corners to exit doors. Distance in feet.

Life Safety / Egress Symbols.description: Wall or barrier with smoke-resistance notation. 'SMOKE BARRIER' or 'SMOKE PARTITION' label.
Life Safety / Egress Symbols.typical_usage: Life safety plans in healthcare, assembly, and corridor separations.
Life Safety / Egress Symbols.vision_hints: Wall with 'SMOKE BARRIER' or 'SM' label. May use different line pattern than fire walls.
